Growth path: serve research teams (ACE, Usher Syndrome, OB labs) with native cost tracking + effort levels for large-scale AI experiments.

top_recommendations.title: Fix Opus 4.8 HTTP 400 Blocker (temperature + effort parameters)
top_recommendations.rationale: June 3 blocker: anthropic_api.py sends temperature field to Opus 4.8 (forbidden when thinking enabled), gets HTTP 400, swallows error body. Prevents 4.6→4.8 generational testing (Mark's experimental baseline). Root cause: adapter built for Opus 4.6 contract (manual thinking budget), not 4.8 (adaptive thinking + output_config.effort). Blocks PROTOCOL-driven research comparisons across models.

top_recommendations.title: Wire effort parameter into API adapters (low/medium/high/xhigh/max)
top_recommendations.rationale: Mark wants to auto-sweep effort levels via rondo_cloud (PROTOCOL.md effort axis), but API path ignores RondoConfig.effort — only CLI /effort works. Blocks automated effort-matrix experiments (single prompt × 3 effort levels × 4 models). Required for Opus 4.8 (output_config.effort), beneficial for cost-quality tradeoff testing on all models.
top_recommendations.source_phase: P1 Usage Mining (Mark's PROTOCOL requirement) + P2 API scan (Opus 4.8 feature)

top_recommendations.title: Implement distributed auto_reconcile locking (INTENT/IN-FLIGHT/DONE atomic transitions)
top_recommendations.rationale: P1 Usage Mining Finding #257 (HIGH by Gemini + Grok): INTENT/IN-FLIGHT/DONE state transitions not atomic. Concurrent workers see stale JSONL view, mark valid in-flight requests as 'stuck', re-dispatch duplicates. Root cause: filesystem-based state machine without distributed locking. Blocks multi-process deployments, limits overnight batch scale (current 4 parallel workers). Affects AuditTrail.auto_reconcile() logic (audit.py lines 558-614).
top_recommendations.source_phase: P1 Usage Mining (Phase D external review, Finding #257)

top_recommendations.title: Stop swallowing HTTP error response bodies in anthropic_api.py
top_recommendations.rationale: June 3 diagnostic blocker: exc.read() called but discarded in HTTPError handler (line 154 of anthropic_api.py). Returns generic 'HTTP 400: Bad Request' instead of provider's detailed JSON error. Mark spent 3x diagnostic effort to reach 'temperature field is forbidden.' Adding exc.read().decode() to except block + logging provides full context. Low-effort, high-value for debugging.
top_recommendations.target_spec: Rondo-STD-108-error-resilience (new req: error response body capture)

top_recommendations.title: Add provider model registry auto-refresh (daily cache + drift report)
top_recommendations.rationale: P2 USH Syndrome friction: Mark manually maintains ~/.rondo/config.toml model names (gpt-4.1 retired Feb 2026, now gpt-5.4-thinking). No auto-detection. Each provider has /v1/models endpoint. Add daily cron (or on-demand CLI) that caches provider model lists, compares against config.toml, emits 'drift report' warning if model names are stale. Prevents 404 failures on next dispatch.
top_recommendations.target_spec: Rondo-REQ-111-smart-dispatch (new req: model registry cache + drift detection)
top_recommendations.source_phase: P2 USH Syndrome (.claude/rules/rondo-ush-profiles.md, low confidence model names)

top_recommendations.title: Capture diagnostic context in error records (stderr, CLI args, env state)
top_recommendations.rationale: P2 Live Runtime: 103 ERR_SUBPROCESS records have cost_usd=0, duration=0.3s (early crash) but no stderr captured. Cannot distinguish: missing claude binary, permission issue, bad args. Error records show error_code only, no diagnostic snippet. Adding stderr capture to error audit records + environment state (AUTH mode, CLAUDECODE check) enables remote debugging without code inspection.
top_recommendations.source_phase: P2 Live Runtime (audit trail analysis, 103 ERR_SUBPROCESS opacity)

top_recommendations.title: Implement HTTP retry + circuit breaker for all provider adapters (parity with ChatCompletions)
top_recommendations.rationale: P1 Usage Mining Finding #234: ChatCompletionsAdapter has full retry/breaker wiring via retry.py + circuit breaker. Gemini + Anthropic adapters have none. Finding #250 (intermittent provider failures) patches via skip-on-transient (masking flakiness). Proper retry with exponential backoff + circuit breaker pattern (OpenCircuit/HalfOpen/Closed states) needed to survive provider hiccups. Blocks reliable overnight batches.

top_recommendations.title: Fix subprocess auth fragility (restore session state between turns)
top_recommendations.rationale: P2 Live Runtime: 113 dispatches (13.3%) fail with ERR_MALFORMED_JSON + 'Not logged in' in raw_output. claude -p subprocess loses auth mid-execution. Affects multi-turn workflows (max_turns=5 setting is ineffective). Root cause: subprocess doesn't re-authenticate or detect auth timeout, continues with stale tokens. Adding session-state validation before each turn + auth refresh loop improves reliability from 68.5% to ~85%.
top_recommendations.source_phase: P2 Live Runtime (13% malformed JSON + 'Not logged in' failures)

top_recommendations.title: Adopt structured logging into dispatch + provider adapters
top_recommendations.rationale: P1 Usage Mining Finding #242: structured_logging.py built but unused. dispatch.py, mcp_dispatch.py, providers.py still use plain logger (no correlation_id, request_id missing in logs). Finding #259: AuditRecord missing request_id. Wiring structured logger with correlation_id into core dispatch path enables: request tracing, cross-provider log correlation, audit event sequencing. Unlocks Feature Gap: distributed tracing for multi-hop workflows.

top_recommendations.title: Add cost field population on error/timeout paths (not just done status)
top_recommendations.rationale: P2 Live Runtime: 164 error records have cost_usd=0, input_tokens=0 even when duration_sec > 0. Dispatch encountered provider failure but cost impact is invisible. dispatch.py only populates cost on successful API response (line 351), skips on error path. Adding cost capture from error responses (if provider returns tokens before error) + timeout-based cost estimation (min per-request overhead per provider) improves cost accuracy for retry decisions.
top_recommendations.target_spec: Rondo-STD-105-ai-operations (req 012-015: cost tracking on all paths)
top_recommendations.source_phase: P2 Live Runtime (error cost tracking gaps)

top_recommendations.title: Implement distributed idempotency cache (cross-process request deduplication)
top_recommendations.rationale: P1 Usage Mining Finding #241: idempotency.py cache is in-process only. Multi-process or distributed deployment re-dispatches duplicates on retries. Spec: add optional shared cache backend (Redis, SQLite, DynamoDB). Default: in-process (current). When multi-process enabled, check distributed cache before dispatching. Blocks scale-up of overnight batches beyond 4 workers.

top_recommendations.title: Add query optimization for audit trail (JSONL indexing + fast path for recent records)
top_recommendations.rationale: P1 Usage Mining: load_history() scans entire JSONL file (~847 records). For large-scale overnight batches (1000+ dispatches/night), full-file scans become slow. Adding optional SQLite index (task_name, model, status, date) + lazy-load option enables fast queries. Keep JSONL as append-only source-of-truth; index as read-side optimization. Blocks dashboard/monitoring use cases.
top_recommendations.target_spec: Rondo-REQ-104-dispatch-history (new req: index layer + query performance)

top_recommendations.title: Eliminate cyclic imports in MCP module family (fix Finding #254)
top_recommendations.rationale: P1 Usage Mining Finding #254: 11 cyclic dependencies remain in rondo/src despite RONDO-209 'fix' claim. Phase D reviewers flagged MEDIUM risk for multi-process/deployment stress. Root cause: mcp_dispatch imports dispatch which imports mcp_dispatch (indirect cycle). dispatch_routing.py extracted (RONDO-279) reduced risk but didn't eliminate. Adding import-order enforcement (test_conventions.py + tox hook) + breaking cycles by inversion (leaf-module-only imports in dispatch_routing) prevents future regressions.
top_recommendations.source_phase: P1 Usage Mining (Finding #254, Phase D review)

top_recommendations.title: Implement provider dependency + timeout handling with explicit failure modes
top_recommendations.rationale: P2 USH Syndrome: April 20 rondo-hypotheses-review—Mistral did not dispatch (silent absence). Mark got 2 of 3 providers. Rondo should: (a) log why Mistral failed, (b) auto-retry with exponential backoff, (c) return partial results + explicit 'provider X missing' flag, (d) offer count adjustment if some fail. Blocks reliable multi-provider reviews for research.

top_recommendations.rationale: P2 USH Syndrome PROTOCOL.md: Mark manually estimates cost before running. stability_actions.action: Fix subprocess auth state recovery (session validation + token refresh between turns).
stability_actions.rationale: 13.3% malformed JSON failures ('Not logged in'). Before each turn, validate claude -p session is authenticated. If not, run /login silently or re-spawn subprocess. Currently max_turns=5 is ineffective due to auth loss. Add session_is_valid() helper + auth_refresh_if_needed() before turn N>1.

stability_actions.action: Implement HTTP retry with exponential backoff for Gemini + Anthropic adapters (parity with ChatCompletions).
stability_actions.rationale: Finding #234 showed ChatCompletionsAdapter has full retry wiring; others don't. Finding #250 (intermittent provider failures) is masked by skip-on-transient. Proper retry (3 attempts, 1s/4s/16s backoff, jitter) + circuit breaker pattern (trip on 5 consecutive failures, half-open after 60s) needed for production reliability.

stability_actions.action: Add structured logging with correlation_id to dispatch.py + provider adapters (Finding #242 adoption).
stability_actions.rationale: structured_logging.py is built but unused. Core dispatch paths still use plain logger. Wire structured logger with correlation_id (generate UUID per dispatch_id) into mcp_dispatch._rondo_run_inner + all provider.dispatch() calls. Enables request tracing + audit event sequencing.

quick_wins: Remove hardcoded [:2000] truncation in mcp_dispatch.py (Finding #258 — buried response slice breaking Phase D). Single-line delete.
Add exc.read().decode() + logging to HTTPError handler in anthropic_api.py. Exposes full error bodies (Opus 4.8 'temperature forbidden' now visible). 3-line fix.
Lazily-load dispatch_routing + round_loader in allowed_imports allowlist (test_conventions.py) to prevent future Finding #271-style violations. 2-line change.
Auto-populate request_id in error records (audit.py, lines 322-326 already propagate for DONE; extend to error/blocked status). Enables cross-retry tracing for all status types.

Add cost_usd field population on error path using minimum provider overhead estimate (vs current 0). dispatch.py lines 629-630: estimate = min_cost_per_request.get(provider, 0.001). Improves cost visibility for retry budgeting.
Update RONDO-REFERENCE.md CLI command count (RONDO-290 added version --bump, updated count from 26→27 commands). Documentation drift fix.
Backfill request_id for old audit records (retroactive in-place update of rondo_audit.jsonl via migration script). Takes <10s, improves traceability for historical replay.
